title: Calculating the internal bacterial load of Drosophila
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"chjkp","text":"This protocol is part of the manuscript: Gonçalves et al. Commensal bacteria and essential amino acids control food choice behavior and reproduction. Plos Biology. 2017 Apr 18.\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":41,"length":135}],"data":[]},{"key":"1ej20","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B492FAD39B2F47548AB135702764A54B","url":"http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2000862"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"air14","text":"All reagents (1x PBS) and materials (pestles, beads) used in this protocol need to be sterile.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare enough MRS, mannitol and LB plates to perform at least 3 replicates of at least 2 dilutions per condition, according to the Guidelines in Growing Drosophila gut bacteria protocol.
Take these Eppendorf tubes to the flow hood and process all conditions in parallel. The next steps are performed in a laminar flow hood and all reagents and material need to be sterile.
Add 70% ethanol to the Eppendorf tube and invert it multiple times during one minute to surface sterilize the flies. 00:01:00
Add sterile 1x PBS, mix well and remove.

Add 100 µl of 1x PBS to each Eppendorf tube (EppA) and homogenize using a sterile pestle until there are no intact flies and the sample looks homogeneous. We start the homogenization with a smaller volume so that the PBS does not spill out of the Eppendorf tube.
Wait that the debris sink. This can take up to 5-10 minutes. Use this time to process the next conditions. 00:05:00
Do serial dilutions (1:100 and 1:1000) for each condition as follows in the next steps.
To make a 1:100 dilution, pipette 100 μl from EppB tube to a 15 ml Falcon tube and add 9900 ul of 1x PBS.
To inoculate the plates transfer 50 μl solution from the corresponding Falcon tube onto the plate making sure the liquid is distributed over its surface.
Transfer sterile glass beads to the plates, stack 4-5 plates containing the beads and shake them vigorously in all horizontal directions until the sample covers the plate surface evenly.
Remove the beads by just opening the lid the minimum amount necessary to avoid contamination.
Repeat steps 17-19 in this section for all conditions/dilutions.

Incubate MRS plates at 37ºC for 48h, LB plates at 37ºC for 24h, and Mannitol plates at 30º C for 24h. It is advisable to check the plates before the full time has elapsed to avoid an overgrowth of the culture due to a high amount of colonies which could preclude counting separate colonies. Much longer incubation times can lead to the appearance of unspecific colonies from other than the 5 bacteria being surveyed by this protocol. This is especially the case when using non gnotobiotic flies.

Count the number of colonies in each plate making sure that the plate only contains separated, well defined single colonies. Calculate the CFU/fly for every plate using the following formulas: CFU/ml = ((total number of colonies on a plate) * (dilution factor (100 or 1000) * 1,8)) / (plated volume) CFU/fly = ((CFU/ml) * total volume in EppA)) / (number of flies per condition)
Add 400 μl of 1x PBS to each Eppendorf tube for a total volume of 500 μl of sample.

Pipette 250 μl of supernatant from the EppA solution into EppB and add 200 μl of 1x PBS to EppB. If you are not able to pipette 250 μl of the samples without taking a lot of debris, you should wait until the samples settle a bit more.
At this stage every EppB should contain the final volume of 450 μl.
creator: Carlos Ribeiro
